title: The bear stays
summary: A mother and her ten-year-old son sort old toys to give away. He keeps one — and asks his mother a small question back.

body.speaker: Maria
body.text: Tobias, sit. We need to look at this box.

body.speaker: Tobias
body.text: I know what is in it. My old toys.

body.speaker: Maria
body.text: The wooden train. The fire engine. The bear with one ear. You haven't played with them for three years.

body.speaker: Tobias
body.text: I know.

body.speaker: Maria
body.text: We can give them to children who do not have toys.

body.speaker: Tobias
body.text: Yes. Mostly.

body.speaker: Maria
body.text: 'Mostly'?

body.speaker: Tobias
body.text: Not the bear.

body.speaker: Maria
body.text: Tell me about the bear.

body.speaker: Tobias
body.text: Grandma gave me the bear when I was four. He has one ear because I bit it.

body.speaker: Maria
body.text: Why?

body.speaker: Tobias
body.text: I was angry. I do not remember why.

body.speaker: Maria
body.text: Many children will love a bear with one ear.

body.speaker: Tobias
body.text: Mum, I know. But this bear remembers I bit him. Another bear will not remember anything.

body.speaker: Maria
body.text: … that is a serious reason.

body.speaker: Tobias
body.text: I will give the train. I will give the fire engine. I will give all the cars. I will not give the bear.

body.speaker: Maria
body.text: Okay. The bear stays.

body.speaker: Tobias
body.text: Mum, do you have a thing you cannot give?

body.speaker: Maria
body.text: I have a small blue cup. From my mother.

body.speaker: Tobias
body.text: Show me one day.

body.speaker: Maria
body.text: One day.

body.speaker: Tobias
body.text: Okay. We can pack the box now.

annotations.meaningVi: chưa/không chơi với — cấu trúc hiện tại hoàn thành phủ định đi kèm khoảng thời gian ('for three years')
phần lớn, hầu hết — trạng từ chỉ mức độ
đã cho tôi — quá khứ đơn của 'give someone something'
tôi không nhớ — mẫu câu thường dùng để nói về ký ức bị mờ nhạt
sẽ không nhớ bất cứ điều gì — phủ định tương lai với 'anything'
thứ mà bạn không thể cho đi — mệnh đề quan hệ rút gọn (không có 'that/which')
Một ngày nào đó hãy cho tôi xem — 'one day' nghĩa là 'vào một ngày nào đó trong tương lai'
cái cốc nhỏ màu xanh — ví dụ về thứ tự tính từ: kích thước trước màu sắc
ở lại, không bị mang đi — động từ 'stay' ở ngôi thứ ba số ít hiện tại

grammarNotes.title: Future with 'will' for decisions made at the moment of speaking
grammarNotes.bodyVi: A2 dùng 'will + động từ' để công bố một quyết định ngay lúc nói. Trong bài: 'I will give the train. I will give the fire engine. I will give all the cars. I will not give the bear.' Đây không phải dự báo (Mr Edvard's case), cũng không phải lên kế hoạch từ trước. Tobias đang quyết định trong lúc nói. Lưu ý phủ định 'will not' (won't) mạnh và rõ — cậu khẳng định không cho con gấu.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: A2 uses 'will + verb' to announce a decision at the moment of speaking. The dialogue: 'I will give the train. I will give the fire engine. I will give all the cars. I will not give the bear.' This is not prediction, not a pre-made plan — Tobias is deciding as he speaks. The negative 'will not' (won't) is firm and clear.

translationVi:
Maria: Tobias, ngồi đi. Mình cần xem cái hộp này.
Tobias: Con biết bên trong có gì. Đồ chơi cũ của con.
Maria: Cái tàu gỗ. Xe cứu hoả. Con gấu một tai. Ba năm rồi con không chơi.
Tobias: Con biết.
Maria: Mình có thể cho mấy bé không có đồ chơi.
Tobias: Vâng. Phần lớn.
Maria: 'Phần lớn'?
Tobias: Không phải con gấu.
Maria: Kể mẹ nghe về con gấu.
Tobias: Bà cho con khi con bốn tuổi. Nó có một tai vì con cắn.
Maria: Sao con cắn?
Tobias: Con tức. Con không nhớ vì sao.
Maria: Nhiều bé sẽ yêu một con gấu một tai.
Tobias: Mẹ, con biết. Nhưng con gấu này nhớ con đã cắn nó. Một con gấu khác sẽ không nhớ gì.
Maria: … đó là một lý do nghiêm túc.
Tobias: Con sẽ cho cái tàu. Con sẽ cho xe cứu hoả. Con sẽ cho tất cả ô tô. Con sẽ không cho con gấu.
Maria: Được. Con gấu ở lại.
Tobias: Mẹ, mẹ có thứ gì mẹ không cho được không?
Maria: Mẹ có một cái cốc xanh nhỏ. Của bà ngoại.
Tobias: Một ngày mẹ cho con xem.
Maria: Một ngày.
Tobias: Được. Giờ mình đóng hộp.

questions.prompt: What toys are mentioned by name?
options: The wooden train, the fire engine, the bear with one ear
A red ball, a doll, a kite

questions.prompt: How long has Tobias not played with them?
questions.explanation: 'You haven't played with them for three years.'

questions.prompt: Why does the bear have only one ear?
options: Tobias bit it when he was four (he was angry).
questions.explanation: 'He has one ear because I bit it.'

questions.prompt: Why does Tobias want to keep the bear?
options: This bear remembers he bit him; another bear will not remember anything.
He has not finished playing with it.
questions.hint: Look at Tobias's reasoning about memory.

questions.prompt: How does Maria react to his reason?
options: She tells him to give the bear anyway.
She gives him a new bear.

questions.prompt: Which toys WILL Tobias give away?
options: The train, the fire engine, and all the cars — but not the bear

questions.prompt: What does Tobias ask his mother in return?
options: Whether she has a thing she cannot give.
Whether she will buy him a new toy.
Whether the bear can come to school.
questions.explanation: 'Mum, do you have a thing you cannot give?'

options: A small blue cup from her mother
questions.explanation: 'I have a small blue cup. From my mother.'

questions.prompt: What is the main idea of the dialogue?
options: Some objects are kept because they remember us back — and a child can teach a parent that they have their own version of the same thing.
Children should give all their toys away.
Bears should never be given to other children.
questions.explanation: Tobias's 'this bear remembers I bit him' and Maria's blue cup are both 'cannot-give' objects.
questions.hint: Notice how the conversation moves from child to mother.

Tobias: I know what is in it. My old {{b1}}.
Maria: The wooden train. The fire engine. The bear with one ear. You haven't played with them for {{b2}} years.
Tobias: I know.
Maria: We can give them to children who do not have toys.
Tobias: Yes. {{b3}}.
Maria: 'Mostly'?
Tobias: Not the bear.
Maria: Tell me about the bear.
Tobias: Grandma gave me the bear when I was {{b4}}. He has one ear because I bit it.
Maria: Why?
Tobias: I was {{b5}}. I do not remember why.
Maria: Many children will love a bear with one ear.
Tobias: Mum, I know. But this bear {{b6}} I bit him. Another bear will not remember anything.
Maria: … that is a {{b7}} reason.
Tobias: I will give the train. I will give the fire engine. I will give all the cars. I will not give the {{b8}}.
Maria: Okay. The bear stays.
Tobias: Mum, do you have a thing you cannot {{b9}}?
Maria: I have a small blue cup. From my mother.
Tobias: Show me one day.
Maria: One day.
Tobias: Okay. We can pack the box {{b10}}.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Tobias keeps the bear because 'this bear remembers I bit him.' Is there an object in your own life that knows something about you that no other version of the object could know? What would you tell your child if they asked you the same question Tobias asked his mother?
